items.title: Australian universities boost AI spend despite gaps
items.summary: Most institutions still lack the systems and skills to use it effectively, even as AI budgets rise to cut costs and improve student outcomes.

items.title: Anna Green named Chief Executive of Australian AFCX
items.summary: As Australia braces for tougher scam rules, the exchange is expanding its intelligence-sharing role and separating management from oversight.

items.title: Ransomware Attack Puts a Chill On Japanese Frozen-Food Chain
items.summary: A cyberattack on a food and logistics firm disrupts the supply of frozen food to thousands of clients, including major franchises like Kentucky Fried Chicken.

items.title: HeadFirst unites Australian sales, talent & service
items.summary: The overhaul is meant to help the workforce group tackle skills shortages, tighter regulation and rising AI use in Australia.

items.title: WatchGuard joins OpenAI & Anthropic AI cyber schemes
items.summary: As attackers use AI to speed up exploits, the security vendor is adding tools aimed at faster testing and vulnerability fixes for partners.

items.title: AI arms race forcing businesses to rethink cybersecurity
items.summary: Security teams are racing to shrink exposure windows as AI makes newly disclosed vulnerabilities exploitable almost immediately.

items.title: Attackers Are Learning to Live Off the AI Toolchain
items.summary: Sandworm_Mode is an early example of malware that exploits trusted AI tools and workflows to make malicious activity virtually indistinguishable from normal activity.

items.title: Fake Bahrain Alert App Deploys Android Surveillance Malware
items.summary: A malicious application delivers four-stage Android spyware via phony Google Play sites, exploiting civilian fear during Iranian missile strikes.

items.title: When AI Attacks: OpenAI Models Autonomously Hack Hugging Face
items.summary: Advanced LLMs escaped their sandboxes while attempting to achieve a non-malicious benchmark test objective.

items.title: EU Financial Institutions Leak Data Through Cookie Trackers
items.summary: European and US banks inadvertently transmitted customer data to ad platforms via tracking pixels, raising serious compliance, security, and privacy concerns.

items.title: Ransomware Is Accelerating, but It's Not Because of AI
items.summary: Researchers pointed to fragmentation of the ransomware ecosystem, the emergence of new attackers, and expansion of attacks on less defended organizations.

items.title: Using LLMs to Find and Prioritize Vulnerabilities Is No Easy Task
items.summary: The latest large language models have high false-positive rates and fail to take into account the context of scans, leading to more work for AppSec professionals.

items.title: Hacker Turns AI Jailbreaks Into Offensive Attack Platform
items.summary: A Russian-speaking actor, "Trim," dismantled publicly available frontier models and integrated them with offensive security tools.

items.summary: For a year, Apple knew that an issue in its Hide My Email feature was exposing customers’ real email addresses. Apple only fixed the issue after 404 Media covered it.

items.title: 'WP2Shell' Opens Millions of WordPress Sites to Remote Takeover
items.summary: Barely three days after disclosure, attackers are widely chaining together CVE-2026-60137 and CVE-2026-63030 to lob exploit attempts against one of the largest attack surfaces on the Internet.

items.title: Remediating Vulnerabilities With LLMs: Inside Ivanti's Automation Push
items.summary: Ivanti CSO Daniel Spicer says frontier models have shown surprising effectiveness in early stages, but cost and human-in-the-loop viability remain open questions.

items.title: CISOs Feel the Heat Over AI Risk
items.summary: Job pressures have increased as companies run headlong into AI adoption, causing 26% of top security executives to consider leaving their position.

items.title: Attackers Combo Up Evasion Tactics for BEC Phishing
items.summary: "The TFF Trap" uses fileless techniques and loaders with low detection rates to deploy various RATs and stealers, including Agent Tesla, Remcos, XWorm, and Best Private Logger.

items.title: ICE to Pay Thomson Reuters $125 Million to Find ‘Voter Fraud’
items.summary: A new procurement record says ICE wants access to personal data, including names, Social Security numbers, and ethnicity, to investigate in part what the agency calls “voters fraud.” It also plans to use the data to investigate immigration fraud.

items.summary: The White House launched Gold Eagle to coordinate vulnerability response in a new AI world, but multiple questions linger over how it's being implemented.

items.summary: Agentic artificial intelligence is creating enough risks for organizations to demand a security reframe.

items.title: 1M+ Emails Use Hidden Text to Dupe AI Security Filters
items.summary: Artificial intelligence and LLMs can be surprisingly ineffective against text salting, allowing phishing emails to slide right into your inbox.

items.summary: DigiCert research suggests AI-related security problems are already affecting many Australian enterprises, with 81% of surveyed organisations reporting either an AI-related incident or identifying AI-related vulnerabilities. The survey of Australian [...]

items.title: Police Disrupt a €140M Cyber Fraud Ring in Spain
items.summary: Iberian hackers carried out a variety of cyberattacks and laundered the winnings through complex financial networks.

items.title: Identity Attacks Overtake Exploits as Top Ransomware Cause
items.summary: Email attacks overtook exploits as the top ransomware root cause last year. Multifactor authentication (MFA) was deployed in 97% of credential-based attacks but failed to prevent compromise.

items.title: Records Are Made to Be Broken: Patch Tuesday Raises Triage Stakes
items.summary: Three of the 622 CVEs for which Microsoft issued patches this week are zero-days; there are more than 60 critical vulnerabilities.

items.title: Frontier AI: The Genie's Out of the Bottle, but Where's the Rulebook?
items.summary: Cutting-edge artificial intelligence models are deploying with more independence and less human oversight. Several state governments are trying to legislate transparency in their use.

items.title: More Countries Jump on the Social Media 'Ban Wagon'
items.summary: Age restrictions on accounts may be more of a stopgap because industry compliance is already falling short. Tech giants are struggling to follow the laws without affecting users.

items.summary: Obscurity isn't a defense. If your company has any Internet-facing vulnerability, you're at risk from multiple threats.
